heading: Murrumba Downs vs Mutarnee — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Murrumba Downs, Queensland and Mutarnee, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mutarnee has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Murrumba Downs (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Murrumba Downs or Mutarnee safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Murrumba Downs has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Mutarnee scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Murrumba Downs scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Mutarnee scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Murrumba Downs or Mutarnee?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mutarnee has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
